name: Ariakit
description: Toolkit for building accessible web apps with React using unstyled, primitive components

description.value: Toolkit for building accessible web apps with React using unstyled, primitive components

license.comment: MIT license for packages directory; examples folder is MIT except for 'Plus' examples which require Ariakit Plus customer status

active-maintenance.comment: Active - 5,846 commits, 310+ releases, maintained by Diego Haz and team, frequent updates through 2024-2025

initial-release.comment: Repository created September 19, 2017 (originally as Reakit, renamed to Ariakit)

framework.comment: React-focused; works with React 17 and above; supports Next.js App Router; experimental Solid support in development

styling-approach.comment: Completely unstyled primitive components; compatible with any styling solution including Tailwind CSS, styled-components, CSS modules, inline styles

component-model.comment: Headless component library providing behavior and accessibility without styles; 59+ styled examples available for copy-paste

typescript.comment: 83% TypeScript codebase with first-class type support

ssr-compatible.comment: Works with server-side rendering; supports Next.js App Router

rsc-compatible.comment: Interactive components require 'use client' directive; not natively RSC-compatible as they are client components

design-system.comment: Headless library with no design system; users implement their own styling

dark-mode.comment: Unstyled - dark mode implementation is user's responsibility

theming-system.comment: No built-in theming system - unstyled components allow complete custom theming

css-variables.comment: No CSS variables provided; unstyled library

design-tokens.comment: No design tokens - headless library focused on behavior and accessibility

rtl-support.comment: RTL support via HTML dir attribute; components respond to document direction

component-count.comment: 16 standard components (Button, Checkbox, Combobox, Dialog, Disclosure, Form, Heading, Hovercard, Menu, Menubar, Popover, Radio, Select, Tab, Toolbar, Tooltip, VisuallyHidden) plus 7 abstract components (Collection, Command, Composite, Focusable, Group, Portal, Role, Separator)

text-input.comment: Focused on complex interactive components; uses Form component with native HTML inputs

textarea.comment: Not provided - use native HTML textarea with Form component

select.comment: Select component with SelectProvider, Select, SelectPopover, and SelectItem sub-components

combobox.comment: Full Combobox component with filtering, async transitions, and autocomplete support

radio.comment: Radio component for radio button groups

switch-toggle.comment: Not provided as distinct component; can be implemented with Checkbox

number-input.comment: Not provided - use native HTML number input

input-with-dropdown.comment: Can be built with Combobox or Select components

button-groups.comment: Toolbar component provides grouped button functionality

split-button.comment: Not provided but can be composed with Menu

card.comment: Not provided - layout component beyond primitive scope

modal-dialog.comment: Dialog component with focus trapping, backdrop support, nested dialogs, and animation support

drawer-sheet.comment: Not provided as distinct primitive; Dialog can be styled as drawer

popover.comment: Popover component with Portal, anchor positioning, and focus management

tooltip.comment: Tooltip component with hover hint support

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu component with keyboard navigation, submenus, and menu bar support

context-menu.comment: Menu component can be used as context menu

command-palette.comment: Command Menu example with tabs available; combines Dialog and Combobox for Cmd+K style interface

tabs.comment: Tab component for tab-based content switching

sidebar.comment: Not provided; can be built with Disclosure

form-libraries.comment: Ariakit has its own Form component with useFormStore for built-in state management and validation; can be integrated with React Hook Form via Controller component but no official adapter

animation-libraries.comment: Documentation shows examples with Framer Motion; supports animation via data attributes

icon-libraries.comment: No bundled icons; examples use various icon libraries

date-libraries.comment: No date picker components, so no date library integrations

aria-compliant.comment: Follows WAI-ARIA design patterns with automatic ARIA attributes and keyboard interactions

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard navigation including arrow key navigation, escape key handling, and focus management

screen-reader-tested.comment: Used in production by WordPress Gutenberg and WooCommerce; enables WCAG compliance without deep accessibility expertise

focus-management.comment: Automatic focus management with data-active-item and data-focus-visible attributes; focus trapping in dialogs

wcag-level.comment: Enables developers to meet WCAG standards; used by major accessible projects like WordPress Gutenberg

accessibility-rating.comment: Excellent - Accessibility is the core focus; used by WordPress Gutenberg and WooCommerce; follows WAI-ARIA patterns strictly; comprehensive keyboard and screen reader support

bundle-size-full.comment: ~38 kB gzipped for full library import

bundle-size-core.comment: Individual component imports available; actual bundle depends on components used due to tree-shaking

zero-runtime.comment: JavaScript runtime required for component behavior and accessibility

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be imported individually and code-split

documentation-quality.comment: Good - comprehensive API docs, 59+ styled examples, guides, blog updates; monthly newsletter with 1,000+ subscribers

interactive-docs.comment: Interactive examples with live code in documentation

figma-kit.comment: No official Figma kit - unstyled library without design system

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~386k weekly downloads for @ariakit/react as of December 2025

commercial-support.comment: Ariakit Plus offers premium examples and priority support; no corporate backing but sustainable through paid tier

style-override.comment: Unstyled components accept className and style props; also provides data-* attributes for state-based styling

compound-components.comment: Uses Provider pattern with compound components (e.g., SelectProvider, Select, SelectPopover, SelectItem)

render-props.comment: Supports render prop pattern for customization

slots.comment: React library - uses children composition instead of slots

unstyled-mode.comment: Entirely unstyled by default - headless component library
